title: Pleuropteropyrum iturupense
taxonomy: Pleuropteropyrum iturupense is classified within the family Polygonaceae. It belongs to the genus Pleuropteropyrum, which is a monotypic genus. The species is native to Russia and is a rare alpine plant. It is typically found in rocky areas and is known for its unique appearance. Pleuropteropyrum iturupense is considered an endangered species due to its limited habitat and distribution.

intro: Pleuropteropyrum iturupense, also known as Iturup Island Pleuropteropyrum, is a rare and unique plant species found in the Russian Far East, belonging to the family Polygonaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleuropteropyrum iturupense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate various health conditions.
